# Emre Varol

> Founder & CEO of A2SV (Africa to Silicon Valley), a Google-backed nonprofit transforming Africa's tech ecosystem.

## About

Emre Varol is a Turkish-born software engineer, AI researcher, and social entrepreneur based between Palo Alto, Kigali, and Ankara. He founded A2SV in 2019 after 8+ years in Silicon Valley working at Google, Palantir, and Liftoff.

## A2SV Impact

- 1,200+ engineers trained across 22 African countries
- 120+ job offers at companies like Google, Amazon, and Bloomberg
- 8,500+ hackathon participants from 48 countries
- 20+ products incubated addressing African challenges
- 70% Google interview pass rate for A2SV graduates

## Career Timeline

- **A2SV Foundation & Eskalate** (2019–Present): Founder & CEO
- **Liftoff Mobile** (2016–2019): AI Engineer — ML pipelines, 15% revenue uplift
- **Google Research** (2014–2016): Software Engineer — NLP, ad relevance
- **Palantir** (2012–2014): Software Engineer — Git migration, database optimization
- **Yahoo! Research** (2009–2011): Research Intern — distributed systems

## Education

- M.S. Computer Engineering, Bilkent University (2010–2012, GPA 3.61)
- B.S. Computer Engineering, Bilkent University (2006–2010, GPA 3.64)

## A2SV Products

- **Adot** (adot.life): Maternity companion for African mothers
- **AfroChat** (afrochat.app): AI assistant localized for Africa, 2.6k+ users in 85 countries
- **Akil** (akilconnect.org): Career platform connecting African youth with opportunities
- **Dime** (dime.gold): Money transfer to Africa with best exchange rates
- **Eskalate** (eskalate.io): IT outsourcing with Google-caliber African developers
- **RateEat** (rateeat.app): Restaurant discovery platform for Africa
- **SkillBridge** (skillbridge.academy): AI-powered learning for African high school students

## Awards & Publications

- TOYP (Ten Outstanding Young Persons) 2024
- International Olympiad in Informatics (IOI) participant, 2005
- National Informatics Olympiad champion, Turkey
- IEEEXtreme global top 10
- Published at SIGIR '10 and CIKM '11 (ACM conferences)

## Languages

This website is available in 10 languages: English, Turkish, Spanish, Kinyarwanda, French, German, Arabic, Portuguese, Swahili, and Amharic.
